title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Broken-Build Bay
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a repository engineering contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Use a changed debug case for gates risk contract that tests this competency: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Repo Purpose trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.

content.text: For Repo Purpose, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Repo Purpose precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set.

stimulus.title: Repo Purpose case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed debug case for gates risk contract that tests this competency: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime. Artifact under review: a repository engineering contract.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Repo Purpose, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Repo Purpose record says a repository engineering contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Repo Purpose without its stated preconditions, omit the a repository engineering contract trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Repo Purpose for a repository engineering contract.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-exception-policy-jjk9hw: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Exception Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-exception-policy-jjk9hw:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Exception Policy structural check is only one gate. a repository engineering contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Exception Policy precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case for gates risk contract that tests this competency: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A disabled rule needs no explanation if CI passes.
